source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/08_Cybersecurity/7. A-CLA-HNXR1_Cybersecurity Labeling.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity labeling for the HnX-R1 Digital X-ray Imaging System, including device description, cybersecurity controls, logging, SBOM, update process, backup and incident response instructions.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by providing warnings, precautions, notices and cybersecurity labeling information for HnX-R1 software cyber risks.
keyPoints: Scope is limited to the Digital X-ray Imaging System HnX-R1.
The system includes detector hardware and HnVUE console software for image acquisition and image processing.
Security measures include Windows Defender, network connection allow list rules, ID/password login controls and restricted software installation.
Software and firmware updates cannot be performed in the field and require collection by the manufacturer or service center.
If a cybersecurity threat is detected, users are instructed to disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device and contact the H&abyz service team.

entities.name: Digital X-ray Imaging System
entities.evidence: Product name Digital X-ray Imaging System

entities.name: PACS
entities.evidence: The captured images are sent to the Picture Archiving & Communication System (PACS) server, and can be used for reading images.

entities.name: DICOM
entities.evidence: saved in DICOM file, a standard for medical imaging

entities.name: Cybersecurity test report EBB-2025-000109
entities.evidence: Refer to Cybersecurity test report (Report no. EBB-2025-000109)

concepts.name: Cybersecurity labeling purpose
concepts.evidence: This is a document that has written warnings, precautions, and notices about software cyber risks.

concepts.name: HnX-R1 intended use
concepts.evidence: HnX-R1 (Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Mammography exclusion
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk management
concepts.evidence: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-R1 have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

concepts.name: Allow list and deny list
concepts.evidence: Access to the software can be granted by logging in using an ID and password.

concepts.name: Communication ports
concepts.evidence: In this chapter, all ports of communication are mentioned.

concepts.name: EOL/EOS
concepts.evidence: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS due to the nature of the product.

concepts.name: Software and firmware download process
concepts.evidence: Software (firmware) updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after the product has been collected from the manufacturer or a service center.

concepts.name: Security event response
concepts.evidence: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Documentation document number A-CLA-HNXR1.
The document title is Cybersecurity Labeling.
The document revision is Rev. No. 0.
The document revision date is August 06, 2025.
The cybersecurity labeling scope is limited to the Digital X-ray Imaging System HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: This cybersecurity labeling is limited to the Digital X-ray Imaging System (HnX-R1).

evidence.claim: The HnX-R1 is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-R1 (Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The HnX-R1 is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The system includes detector, power supply box, battery pack, battery charger, accessories and software for image acquisition and processing, and does not include the X-ray generator.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-R1 wired/wireless digital X-ray imaging system consists of Detector, Power supply box, Battery pack, Battery charger, Other Accessories and software for image acquisition and image processing and does not include the X-ray generator.

evidence.claim: Captured images are saved in DICOM file format and sent to a PACS server.
evidence.evidenceText: saved in DICOM file, a standard for medical imaging. The captured images are sent to the Picture Archiving & Communication System (PACS) server

evidence.claim: H&abyz states that HnX-R1 cybersecurity risks have been addressed as part of device risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-R1 have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

evidence.claim: Endpoint protection includes Wi-Fi communication reference testing, Windows Defender anti-malware and network connection whitelist firewall rules.
evidence.evidenceText: Wi-FI communication : Refer to Cybersecurity test report (Report no. EBB-2025-000109) Anti-malware : window defender Firewall / Firewall rules : Network connection white list(ACL)

evidence.claim: Software access is granted by logging in with an ID and password.
evidence.evidenceText: Access to the software can be granted by logging in using an ID and password.

evidence.claim: Unauthorized users cannot log in and software installation is limited to authorized persons.
evidence.evidenceText: Unauthorized users can't login and have limited access to the software. Software installations may only be installed by an authorized person.

evidence.claim: Logs are updated in real time, saved as hidden .txt files and viewed through a log viewer instead of IDS or SIEM automated analysis software.
evidence.evidenceText: The logs are updated in real time. The log file is saved in a .txt format and is hidden to prevent access by general users. The log file is utilized through a log viewer instead of automated analysis software IDS , SIEM

evidence.claim: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS; the end date is determined by software change or the 10-year software life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS due to the nature of the product. The end date is determined by the software change or the software life cycle (10 years).

evidence.claim: Software and firmware updates cannot be performed in the field.
evidence.evidenceText: Software (firmware) updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after the product has been collected from the manufacturer or a service center.

evidence.claim: When a cybersecurity threat is detected, users should disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device and contact H&abyz service.
evidence.evidenceText: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; authoritySignal CE/MDR is metadata only and was not used as a document fact.
Some OCR or source formatting issues are present, including broken table rows, misspellings such as 'unkonown' and inconsistent line breaks in SBOM dates.
Referenced cybersecurity test report EBB-2025-000109 is cited, but its contents are not included in this source text.
